claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm enquiring about my son Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about Alex's current situation:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

(For example, is he in school, college, sixth form, or undertaking an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he's not in school or college anymore. He's started an apprenticeship actually - he's training in a trade.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to clarify something important:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship specifically an apprenticeship in England?**

This is an important distinction for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - it's an apprenticeship here in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 17 years old
- He has started an apprenticeship in England

According to the Child Benefit rules, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. This is specifically stated in the eligibility criteria.

Let me provide my formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England. Although you can normally continue to receive Child Benefit for children under 20 who stay in approved education or training, apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded from this rule.

If you were already receiving Child Benefit for Alex, your payments would have stopped when he began his apprenticeship.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Although children under 20 can qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training, apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded from this provision. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (17 years old), who has started an apprenticeship in England. Under Child Benefit rules, parents stop receiving Child Benefit when their child starts an apprenticeship in England, even if the child is under 20. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Although children under 20 can qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training, apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded from this provision. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (17 years old), who has started an apprenticeship in England. Under Child Benefit rules, parents stop receiving Child Benefit when their child starts an apprenticeship in England, even if the child is under 20. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
